item.title: SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless review: The best gaming headset for a reason
item.itunes:title: SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless review: The best gaming headset for a reason
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Quick Links

  • SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless: Pricing and availability
  • Design
  • Performance and audio quality
  • Software
  • Should you buy the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless?

In the world of gaming headsets, there are so, so many to choose from. I see so many options in the Counter-Strike: Global Offensive scene. I know many people who use a HyperX headset and many more who use a regular pair of headphones and a dedicated microphone. I fall into the latter camp and always have (I previously used Sennheiser Momentum 4 Wireless wired to my PC, for example), but the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless actually managed to change that.

The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless is one of the best gaming headsets because it has so many great features just for gamers regardless of how you like to play. It has great battery life, extremely solid sound quality (not the best, but still quite good), zero latency, and full wireless capability. There are a ton of additional quality-of-life improvements and features that really propel this headset to be one of the best, and I'm in no way surprised that this is the device that frequently tops gaming headset lists.

About this review: SteelSeries sent me the Arctis Nova Pro Wireless for the purposes of this review, and it had no input into its contents.

SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro
Best around
9 / 10

The SteelSeries Arcits Nova Pro is a premium gaming headset. It offers lag-free audio, a retractable microphone, effective ANC, multi-point connectivity, and up to 25 hours of battery life.

Brand
SteelSeries
Battery Life
25 hours
Bluetooth
5.0
Noise Cancellation
Active noise cancellation (ANC)
Waterproof
No
Microphones
Retractable microphone
Colors
Black
Multipoint
Yes
Pros
  • Good sound quality
  • No latency
  • Great customization options
  • Replaceable batteries
Cons
  • Ears can get very warm
  • Software issues with SteelSeries GG client
$350 at Amazon $350 at Best Buy

SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless: Pricing and availability

The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless is available throughout Europe and the U.S., starting at €380, or $350. You can get it just about anywhere, including SteelSeries' website, Amazon, Best Buy, and more.

Design

Very practical

The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless has a pretty standard design, but there are some unique additions. For starters, the headband and ear plates are fully replaceable. I actually received Cherry Red plates from SteelSeries to try this out. Out of the box, they're just gray. On top of that, there's a 3.5mm AUX port for wired audio, a mute button, and a microphone that can be extended or retracted.

Where things get especially unique, though, is under the right ear plate. Here, you'll find a removable battery, and you actually get two of these in the box. The main station that you plug into your PC to transmit audio has a battery charging dock inside of it, so the idea is that you keep one battery in the headset and one in the dock to swap out whenever your headset dies. I've had to do this once in the middle of a game of VALORANT, and it was an amazingly quick process. The battery will last roughly 20 hours on a charge.

The headset itself stays decently secure on my head, and the microphone doesn't tend to get in the way when I'm using it. I love that the microphone can completely retract seamlessly into the body, too, as a lot of people may prefer to use a dedicated microphone instead. There's also a volume slider on the left ear cup.

The only negative thing I have to say about the design is that the earpads, which are quite thick, get quite warm, especially in the summer temperatures. I found myself taking the headphones off every once in a while just because of the warmth, which hasn't been an issue with other headsets and headphones I've used in the past.

The base station that it connects to is very simplistic, with a black and white OLED display and a single rotating dial.

It has two USB-C ports that connect to the device you use the headphones with, and that connects via a USB-A connector. The rotating dial doubles up as a button, and to be honest, it feels somewhat cheaply made. You can change some basic options here, like noise cancelation, and you keep an eye on your battery levels, but you can do basically everything through the SteelSeries client as well, so it's not completely necessary.

Performance and audio quality

Good for a gaming headset

The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless surprised me with its audio quality. I was expecting it to be a decent experience for the price, but I wasn't expecting it to be competitive as just an actually good pair of headphones. It's not the best headset in the world in terms of sound quality, and there are better in this price range, but this is a gaming headset that cares about sound as much as more expensive headsets.

Out of the box, the headset sounds fairly balanced with a slightly increased bass. This is probably a good thing in competitive shooters, as games require a lot of sound, and the bass is probably the first thing you'll hear if an enemy is approaching. But you can configure it further with SteelSeries Sonar, which is a built-in equalizer in the SteelSeries client on Windows.

It's especially impressive since you can process all audio on your PC and even equalize different applications separately. This comes in handy with games like Counter-Strike and VALORANT, which have custom audio profiles designed with pros in mind and sometimes by the pros themselves. However, I've typically found these equalization options gimmicky. I've been playing Counter-Strike for upwards of 10 years, and I usually turn them off. Even in VALORANT, a significantly newer game, I just prefer the regular game audio over the default equalizer.

There are a ton of additional quality-of-life improvements and features that really propel this headset to be one of the best,

The other worry you may have when it comes to a wireless headset is latency. In its 2.4GHz mode, there is no discernible latency, which is exactly what you want for the competitive games this headset is designed for. You can choose to have it operate in distance or speed mode, where speed ensures there is no latency, but it won't travel as far. I can go about 15 or 20 meters away from the docking station with no issues in this mode.

The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless has two major downsides when it comes to audio, though. The first is active noise cancelation (ANC), which works but is fairly weak. It gets the job done if you really want ANC (like playing at a LAN), but you won't get anywhere near the stellar performance of the best active noise-canceling headphones. The second is the SteelSeries client itself, where a crash at one point caused me to lose my audio for several rounds in the middle of a Counter-Strike game.

As for how the microphone sounds, it's decent enough and gets the job done. It's about what you'd expect from a built-in microphone from a gaming headset. I've included an audio clip below.

XDA · SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless Mic Test

Software

Finicky but mostly out of the way

SteelSeries products make use of a program called SteelSeries Engine for controls. I have used multiple SteelSeries products over the years, and one of the first gaming mice that I ever used was the SteelSeries Kana v2. That mouse, even back then, required the SteelSeries Engine. However, since then, the program that you need to use has turned into SteelSeries GG, a rather bloated piece of software that does a lot more than just configure your hardware.

When you open the SteelSeries GG application, it immediately shows you ads for other products. I was greeted with a banner telling me to purchase the Arctis Nova Pro, which is the only SteelSeries product I have connected to my computer. From here, though, there are a number of other options, including Engine, Moments, and Sonar.

Moments is one of the more interesting options that, while bloatware, can be useful. It will clip moments in games for you that you can look back on after, and will integrate with certain games. For example, with Counter-Strike, it defaults to clipping the minute when you get a headshot. It's a bit overzealous in that regard, but you can tweak the integration or just make it so that you manually clip things only. Below is a clip that I took using SteelSeries Moments in VALORANT, but note that the audio from my microphone is not using the Arctis Nova Pro Wireless.

On top of that, you get Sonar, an audio equalizer, and the Engine itself, which can be used for modifying the Nova Wireless.

As mentioned with Sonar, there are existing presets that you can use to apply to games, but these are somewhat gimmicky, and I didn't use them for longer than a couple of hours. Sonar also crashed at one stage and broke my audio in the middle of a game of Counter-Strike, which is obviously a pretty bad time for it to crash. I typically just leave Sonar switched off.

The Engine portion, on the other hand, is pretty basic but gets the job done.

It shows you the current battery level of the headset and all the settings for both the headset and the base station. There are a lot of options to play with here, but I've just basically left on all the defaults. I didn't have any issues with these settings, and everything just works.

Should you buy the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless?

You should buy the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless if:

  • You value audio quality in your games
  • You like a cable-free experience
  • You want a good-quality headset with features that make sense

You shouldn't buy the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless if:

  • You want an excellent microphone (but then you shouldn't get a gaming headset anyway)
  • You want an easy-to-use equalizer in software

As somebody who takes competitive gaming seriously and has taken part in numerous esports tournaments, I'm impressed by the quality of the Arctis Nova Pro Wireless. I can't play with bad audio, and this headset is fantastic, sounds great, and, most of all, packs a lot of useful features that make it easily the best of the best.

The biggest downside of the entire package is the SteelSeries GG client, and that's specifically because of Sonar and how it integrates with audio. The crashes, in particular, are a killer, and a few threads online confirm it wasn't an issue with just my unit. Since disabling it, though, I've been able to use the headset flawlessly. I haven't run into any issues since then, and these headphones are a no-brainer must-buy if you don't care about those kinds of features. I know I don't.

This is simply a phenomenal headset, with a microphone you can easily forget about if you use your own.

SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro
Best around
9 / 10

The SteelSeries Arcits Nova Pro is a premium gaming headset. It offers lag-free audio, a retractable microphone, effective ANC, multi-point connectivity, and up to 25 hours of battery life.

$350 at Amazon $350 at Best Buy
item.description:
Quick Links

  • SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless: Pricing and availability
  • Design
  • Performance and audio quality
  • Software
  • Should you buy the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless?

In the world of gaming headsets, there are so, so many to choose from. I see so many options in the Counter-Strike: Global Offensive scene. I know many people who use a HyperX headset and many more who use a regular pair of headphones and a dedicated microphone. I fall into the latter camp and always have (I previously used Sennheiser Momentum 4 Wireless wired to my PC, for example), but the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless actually managed to change that.

The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless is one of the best gaming headsets because it has so many great features just for gamers regardless of how you like to play. It has great battery life, extremely solid sound quality (not the best, but still quite good), zero latency, and full wireless capability. There are a ton of additional quality-of-life improvements and features that really propel this headset to be one of the best, and I'm in no way surprised that this is the device that frequently tops gaming headset lists.

About this review: SteelSeries sent me the Arctis Nova Pro Wireless for the purposes of this review, and it had no input into its contents.

SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro
Best around
9 / 10

The SteelSeries Arcits Nova Pro is a premium gaming headset. It offers lag-free audio, a retractable microphone, effective ANC, multi-point connectivity, and up to 25 hours of battery life.

Brand
SteelSeries
Battery Life
25 hours
Bluetooth
5.0
Noise Cancellation
Active noise cancellation (ANC)
Waterproof
No
Microphones
Retractable microphone
Colors
Black
Multipoint
Yes
Pros
  • Good sound quality
  • No latency
  • Great customization options
  • Replaceable batteries
Cons
  • Ears can get very warm
  • Software issues with SteelSeries GG client
$350 at Amazon $350 at Best Buy

SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless: Pricing and availability

The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless is available throughout Europe and the U.S., starting at €380, or $350. You can get it just about anywhere, including SteelSeries' website, Amazon, Best Buy, and more.

Design

Very practical

The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless has a pretty standard design, but there are some unique additions. For starters, the headband and ear plates are fully replaceable. I actually received Cherry Red plates from SteelSeries to try this out. Out of the box, they're just gray. On top of that, there's a 3.5mm AUX port for wired audio, a mute button, and a microphone that can be extended or retracted.

Where things get especially unique, though, is under the right ear plate. Here, you'll find a removable battery, and you actually get two of these in the box. The main station that you plug into your PC to transmit audio has a battery charging dock inside of it, so the idea is that you keep one battery in the headset and one in the dock to swap out whenever your headset dies. I've had to do this once in the middle of a game of VALORANT, and it was an amazingly quick process. The battery will last roughly 20 hours on a charge.

The headset itself stays decently secure on my head, and the microphone doesn't tend to get in the way when I'm using it. I love that the microphone can completely retract seamlessly into the body, too, as a lot of people may prefer to use a dedicated microphone instead. There's also a volume slider on the left ear cup.

The only negative thing I have to say about the design is that the earpads, which are quite thick, get quite warm, especially in the summer temperatures. I found myself taking the headphones off every once in a while just because of the warmth, which hasn't been an issue with other headsets and headphones I've used in the past.

The base station that it connects to is very simplistic, with a black and white OLED display and a single rotating dial.

It has two USB-C ports that connect to the device you use the headphones with, and that connects via a USB-A connector. The rotating dial doubles up as a button, and to be honest, it feels somewhat cheaply made. You can change some basic options here, like noise cancelation, and you keep an eye on your battery levels, but you can do basically everything through the SteelSeries client as well, so it's not completely necessary.

Performance and audio quality

Good for a gaming headset

The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless surprised me with its audio quality. I was expecting it to be a decent experience for the price, but I wasn't expecting it to be competitive as just an actually good pair of headphones. It's not the best headset in the world in terms of sound quality, and there are better in this price range, but this is a gaming headset that cares about sound as much as more expensive headsets.

Out of the box, the headset sounds fairly balanced with a slightly increased bass. This is probably a good thing in competitive shooters, as games require a lot of sound, and the bass is probably the first thing you'll hear if an enemy is approaching. But you can configure it further with SteelSeries Sonar, which is a built-in equalizer in the SteelSeries client on Windows.

It's especially impressive since you can process all audio on your PC and even equalize different applications separately. This comes in handy with games like Counter-Strike and VALORANT, which have custom audio profiles designed with pros in mind and sometimes by the pros themselves. However, I've typically found these equalization options gimmicky. I've been playing Counter-Strike for upwards of 10 years, and I usually turn them off. Even in VALORANT, a significantly newer game, I just prefer the regular game audio over the default equalizer.

There are a ton of additional quality-of-life improvements and features that really propel this headset to be one of the best,

The other worry you may have when it comes to a wireless headset is latency. In its 2.4GHz mode, there is no discernible latency, which is exactly what you want for the competitive games this headset is designed for. You can choose to have it operate in distance or speed mode, where speed ensures there is no latency, but it won't travel as far. I can go about 15 or 20 meters away from the docking station with no issues in this mode.

The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless has two major downsides when it comes to audio, though. The first is active noise cancelation (ANC), which works but is fairly weak. It gets the job done if you really want ANC (like playing at a LAN), but you won't get anywhere near the stellar performance of the best active noise-canceling headphones. The second is the SteelSeries client itself, where a crash at one point caused me to lose my audio for several rounds in the middle of a Counter-Strike game.

As for how the microphone sounds, it's decent enough and gets the job done. It's about what you'd expect from a built-in microphone from a gaming headset. I've included an audio clip below.

XDA · SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless Mic Test

Software

Finicky but mostly out of the way

SteelSeries products make use of a program called SteelSeries Engine for controls. I have used multiple SteelSeries products over the years, and one of the first gaming mice that I ever used was the SteelSeries Kana v2. That mouse, even back then, required the SteelSeries Engine. However, since then, the program that you need to use has turned into SteelSeries GG, a rather bloated piece of software that does a lot more than just configure your hardware.

When you open the SteelSeries GG application, it immediately shows you ads for other products. I was greeted with a banner telling me to purchase the Arctis Nova Pro, which is the only SteelSeries product I have connected to my computer. From here, though, there are a number of other options, including Engine, Moments, and Sonar.

Moments is one of the more interesting options that, while bloatware, can be useful. It will clip moments in games for you that you can look back on after, and will integrate with certain games. For example, with Counter-Strike, it defaults to clipping the minute when you get a headshot. It's a bit overzealous in that regard, but you can tweak the integration or just make it so that you manually clip things only. Below is a clip that I took using SteelSeries Moments in VALORANT, but note that the audio from my microphone is not using the Arctis Nova Pro Wireless.

On top of that, you get Sonar, an audio equalizer, and the Engine itself, which can be used for modifying the Nova Wireless.

As mentioned with Sonar, there are existing presets that you can use to apply to games, but these are somewhat gimmicky, and I didn't use them for longer than a couple of hours. Sonar also crashed at one stage and broke my audio in the middle of a game of Counter-Strike, which is obviously a pretty bad time for it to crash. I typically just leave Sonar switched off.

The Engine portion, on the other hand, is pretty basic but gets the job done.

It shows you the current battery level of the headset and all the settings for both the headset and the base station. There are a lot of options to play with here, but I've just basically left on all the defaults. I didn't have any issues with these settings, and everything just works.

Should you buy the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless?

You should buy the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless if:

  • You value audio quality in your games
  • You like a cable-free experience
  • You want a good-quality headset with features that make sense

You shouldn't buy the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless if:

  • You want an excellent microphone (but then you shouldn't get a gaming headset anyway)
  • You want an easy-to-use equalizer in software

As somebody who takes competitive gaming seriously and has taken part in numerous esports tournaments, I'm impressed by the quality of the Arctis Nova Pro Wireless. I can't play with bad audio, and this headset is fantastic, sounds great, and, most of all, packs a lot of useful features that make it easily the best of the best.

The biggest downside of the entire package is the SteelSeries GG client, and that's specifically because of Sonar and how it integrates with audio. The crashes, in particular, are a killer, and a few threads online confirm it wasn't an issue with just my unit. Since disabling it, though, I've been able to use the headset flawlessly. I haven't run into any issues since then, and these headphones are a no-brainer must-buy if you don't care about those kinds of features. I know I don't.

This is simply a phenomenal headset, with a microphone you can easily forget about if you use your own.

SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro
Best around
9 / 10

The SteelSeries Arcits Nova Pro is a premium gaming headset. It offers lag-free audio, a retractable microphone, effective ANC, multi-point connectivity, and up to 25 hours of battery life.

$350 at Amazon $350 at Best Buy

item.title: What are headphone volume notifications on iOS & should you use them?
item.itunes:title: What are headphone volume notifications on iOS & should you use them?
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Great headphones and excellent earbuds are ubiquitous in 2023, and they're more powerful than ever. That's great for people who love watching media and listening to music on-the-go, but it can be bad news for your hearing. If you listen to headphones for too long at high volumes, you can cause temporary or permanent hearing damage. As part of Apple's recent sweeping health initiatives, it introduced headphone safety notifications on iPhone and Apple Watch, and you can also find them on the upcoming iOS 17 and watchOS 10 updates.

When Apple detects that you've been listening to loud music or media for too long, iOS or watchOS can send you notifications that recommend turning your volume down. If you're particularly concerned about your hearing, your iPhone or Apple Watch can automatically lower your volume when it exceeds a certain decibel level. Here's how the little-known Apple Health feature works on iOS and watchOS, and whether you should use it.

What is considered too loud?

Expert opinions vary on how loud your headphone volume should be, but most agree it should be between 60 and 85 decibels. Apple sets this threshold at 80 decibels, so listening to music or audio louder than that will result in headphone volume notifications. Notably, phone calls are excluded from the headphone notifications feature.

How do headphone notifications work?

3 Images

iOS and watchOS can figure out if your headphones have been loud enough to affect your hearing by determining the decibel level associated with your volume. If your headphone volume exceeds 80 decibels for 40 hours total within the last week, Apple will show you a headphone volume notification. This message recommends you turn down the volume, and will turn down the volume the next time you connect your headphones. However, you will still be free to turn up the volume after the fact. For a more proactive solution, you can turn on the Reduce Loud Sounds feature, which will automatically lower your volume when it exceeds the decibel limit of your choice.

How to turn on Reduce Loud Sounds

If you want to set a decibel limit for your headphone use, you can do so with a few taps in the Settings app on your iPhone.

  1. Open the Settings app on iPhone.
  2. Tap the Sound & Haptics tab.
  3. Press the Headphone Safety tab under the Headphone Audio section.
  4. Tap the Reduce Loud Sounds toggle and drag the slider to select your preferred volume limit.
    4 Images

Should you use headphone volume notifications?

This health feature isn't as flashy as ones like Fall Detection or Crash Detection, but it aims to preserve your hearing, which is extremely important. Headphone volume notifications are turned on by default, and some regions require them to remained turned on in accordance with local laws and regulations. These insights are a good way for you to stay in tune with your hearing health, and leaving them on can't hurt. Enabling the Reduce Loud Sounds option can be a different story, since having your volume drop automatically can be frustrating at times. However, it's great that these options exist, even if you don't use them all the time.

item.title: Best Samsung Galaxy A53 screen protectors in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best Samsung Galaxy A53 screen protectors in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
The Samsung Galaxy A53 is one of the best budget Android phones you can buy despite being over a year old. With its premium design, huge 5,000mAh battery, surprisingly solid 64MP rear camera, and five years of guaranteed software updates, the A53 has a lot going on, especially now that you can get it on sale more often.

One of the device's other big highlights is the 6.5-inch Super AMOLED 120Hz display, which stands out as one of the best for under $500. Obviously, if you pick up a Galaxy A53, you'll want to protect that gorgeous panel, and applying a screen protector is a great way to do it. Below, we've rounded up some of the best screen protectors you can get for the A53.

  • Source: Spigen
    Spigen Tempered Glass Galaxy A53 screen protector
    Editor's pick

    Spigen's Tempered Glass screen protector helps retain display responsiveness and comes with an oleophobic coating to mitigate fingerprints. The protector comes with a tempered glass followed by a Matte finish. With an alignment tool in the box, you’ll get the job done in the first attempt.

    $10 at Amazon
  • Source: KATIN
    KATIN Galaxy A53 screen protector
    Best value

    KATIN's tempered glass screen protector for the A53 comes with anti-scratch and fingerprint-resistant coatings. It's also designed to minimize bubbling during installation. One really cool thing about this one is that it fits perfectly even when you have a case on your phone, thanks to the tiny space around the bezel. Apart from that, it has ultra-thin thickness to ensure there’s no compromise on your touch interface. 99.9% transparency helps to enhance your screen view.

    $5 at Amazon
  • Source: ZenHoo
    ZenHoo Galaxy A53 screen protector
    Anti blue light

    Looking for a screen protector that does not hurt your eyes? This one comes with an anti-blue light glass which helps to block ultraviolet and blue light rays. As a result, you’ll experience better screen viewing with minimal strain and fatigue to the eyes. Besides, the protector has a precise fit for Samsung A53 to ensure accuracy at every corner. This one is particularly recommended for individuals who wear glasses.

    $9 at Amazon
  • Source: VISEUP
    VIESUP Galaxy A53 screen protector
    Enhanced privacy

    This innovative protector from Viseup ensures that individuals seated to your left or right won't be able to sneak a peek at your phone's content. However, please note that fingerprint recognition won't function with this protector as it dims out the sensor that scans your fingerprints. You may need to adjust your screen-lock method or consider an alternative if this is a crucial feature for you. Nonetheless, if privacy is your biggest concern, get this without a second thought!

    $13 at Amazon
  • Source: Supershieldz
    Supershieldz Galaxy A53 screen protector
    Comes with hydrophobic coating

    This screen protector from Supershieldz is made from tempered glass to ensure maximum protection for your A53. With a hydrophobic coating, there’s going to be minimal hassle even if the phone is wet. For added protection, there is an anti-scratch coating followed by rounded edges that'll sit nicely on your A53.

    $8 at Amazon
  • Source: Otterbox
    OtterBox Galaxy A53 screen protector
    Premium pick

    If you want the best of the best, go for the Otterbox protector. There are three variations to choose from: Alpha Glass, Performance Glass, and Trusted Glass. The Alpha Glass offers top-level protection for your A53 with 5 times better scratch resistance than other protectors. The Performance Glass is specifically designed to block blue light and protect your eyes. The Trusted Glass is a base variant but still offers pristine clarity. Keep in mind that all three of these have a gloss finish.

    $30 at Amazon
  • Source: seninhi
    seninhi Galaxy A53 screen protector
    6-in-1 package

    This Galaxy A53 Screen Protector is designed with precision to match your phone's curved edges, ensuring a snug fit without air bubbles and preserving the phone's sleek appearance. With its crystal-clear, high-grade glass and bubble-free installation, it enhances your visual experience by restoring vibrant colors. It’s an excellent value choice as you’ll be getting a bundle of 6 protectors: 3 for the screen and 3 for the rear camera.

    $10 at Amazon
  • Source: amFilm
    amFilm Galaxy A53 screen protector
    Ultra-slim thickness
    $9 $11 Save $2

    Featuring an ultra-slim 0.33mm thickness, this protector enhances smooth touchscreen responsiveness. You’ll experience exceptional clarity with 99.9% transparency delivering a natural viewing experience. On top of that, this screen protector is highly scratch-resistant with a surface hardness of 9H. The oleophobic coating also reduces fingerprint smudges, and obviously, that matters when you are trying to get your cell a sleek look. The entire package comprises three screen protectors, wet wipes, an easy installation guide, and dust removal stickers.

    $9 at Amazon

Recapping the best Galaxy A53 5G screen protectors

My top pick from this list of screen protectors for the Galaxy A53 would be Spigen's Tempered Glass option. I've been using Spigen screen protectors for as long as I can remember, and they make it extremely easy to install thanks to their bundled guidance tool. Plus, they're high quality and age beautifully. However, if your concern is something different, such as preventing eye strain or blocking of UV light rays, opt for the ZenHoo screen protector. It will allow you to increase your screen usage without any strain on your eyes, thanks to the UV-blocking tampered glass.

If you're looking for more on the Galaxy A53, check out our hands-on, where we go over everything there is to know about it. We also have a roundup of the best cases for the A53.

Samsung Galaxy A53
$300 $450 Save $150

With its stunning Super AMOLED display and powerful Exynos chipset, the Samsung A53 offers a fine smartphone experience. It's got a long-lasting 5000 mAh battery that can keep up with your busy day. Plus, it's rugged too, with IP67 water resistance. Not bad for a mid-range smartphone!

$450 at Samsung $300 at Amazon

item.title: Best accessories for the Nothing Phone 1 in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best accessories for the Nothing Phone 1 in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Although the Nothing Phone 1 isn't the best smartphone Nothing has to offer anymore, it's still a capable device in 2023. The Nothing Phone 2, released in July, took that crown with an improved processor and camera system. However, you can make your Phone 1 better than ever with a few neat accessories. Aside from putting on a case or screen protector for some added protection, you can add some of the best chargers, cables, and earbuds to your Phone 1 for a much better experience. With Nothing OS 2.0 headed to Phone 1 right now, your phone's internal software is getting a refresh, so all that's left for you to do is refresh your accessory portfolio.

Since your phone is probably a year old at this point, getting a new case can make your phone feel brand new. With its semitransparent design, the Nothing Phone 1 lets you see some of its internals, including the wireless charging coil and screws. This transparent design also makes the phone more prone to scratches, fingerprints, and smudges. That's why it's a great idea to add a case to your Phone 1, or grab a new one if your current case is starting to show some wear and tear. To help you out, we've compiled a list of the best cases and accessories for the Nothing Phone 1 below.

  • Osophter Glyph Nothing Phone 1 Case
    Protective case
    £8 at Amazon
  • DU DUICS clear Nothing Phone 1 case
    Anti-yellowing case
    £8 at Amazon
  • SPGKA clear Nothing Phone 1 Case
    Best value
    £5 at Amazon
  • Draamvole Leather Flip Nothing Phone 1 Case
    Folio case
    £9 at Amazon

    Great screen protector
    $7 at Amazon

    Premium earbuds
    $149 at Nothing

    Budget earbuds
    $64 at Amazon

    Versatile earbuds
    $170 at Amazon
  • Google Pixel Watch
    Best smartwatch
    $310 at Amazon
  • iOttie Easy One Touch 4
    Great car mount
    $25 at Amazon
  • Samsung Wireless Charger Duo
    Dual wireless charger
    $43 at Amazon
  • Anker 313 Wireless Charging Stand
    Great charging stand
    $19 at Amazon
  • Belkin BoostCharge Pro Flex USB C to USB C Cable
    Best charging cable
    $26 at Amazon
  • Nothing USB-C Cable
    Fashionable charging cable
    $15 at Nothing
  • Anker 511 20W Nano Pro charger
    Best wall adapter
    $14 at Amazon

  • Source: Anker

    High-capacity power bank

item.title: Best laptops at Best Buy in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best laptops at Best Buy in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Best Buy is one of the best places to go if you want to buy any kind of electronic device, including laptops. Not only does the company offer a huge selection of products, but its Price Match Guarantee helps you get the best deal. There are some exceptions to that, but regardless, this makes Best Buy a compelling place to visit if you want to get the best deal on a new laptop. Not only are you getting them from a reputable retailer, but you can also get them at one of the lowest prices available on the market.

While Best Buy does have a large selection of products, not every laptop on our best laptops list is available there. Generally, it's the more business-oriented products that may not show up there. For one reason or another, there isn't a limitless selection, and you'll still see laptops with RTX 40-series graphics cards, for example. So, in this round-up, we've gathered the best laptops you can buy at Best Buy.

  • Apple MacBook Pro (2023)
    The best overall laptop at Best Buy
    $1799 at Best Buy (14 inches)
  • Razer Blade 16
    Best gaming laptop
    $3000 at Best Buy
  • HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
    Best HP laptop
    $1500 at Best Buy
  • Dell XPS 13 Plus
    Best Dell laptop
    $1303 at Best Buy FHD
  • MacBook Air (M2)
    The best Apple laptop
    $899 at Best Buy (13 inches)
  • Lenovo Yoga 9i (2023)
    Best Lenovo laptop
    $1400 at Best Buy (2.8K OLED)
  • Source: Asus
    Asus ROG Zephyrus M16 (2023)
    Best Asus laptop
    $1500 at Best Buy (RTX 4070)
  • Acer Chromebook Vero 514
    Best laptop under $500
    $500 at Best Buy
  • Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
    Best Samsung laptop
    $1264 at Best Buy (14 inches)

The top laptops available at Best Buy in 2023

Apple MacBook Pro (2023)
The best overall laptop at Best Buy

For those who need the best possible laptop

$1799 $1999 Save $200

The best possible laptop you can buy at Best Buy is the Apple MacBook Pro (2023). This laptop comes in 14-inch and 16-inch sizes, and comes with the M2 Pro or M2 Max chips, ideal for video and photo editing, and for those who own other Apple devices.

Pros
  • The Apple M2 chip is powerful
  • Great for those who own other Apple devices
  • A great display and design
Cons
  • Not for those who like Windows
  • Screen has a notch
$1799 at Best Buy (14 inches) $2299 at Best Buy (16 inches)

The best possible laptop you can find at Best Buy is also one of the most popular. It's the Apple MacBook Pro (2023). Though this laptop has a high cost, it packs plenty of value for the money. The laptop comes in both 14-inch and 16-inch sizes, for one, has an Arm-based SoC under the hood, built and designed by Apple. Even the display is great, though there's a notch. Of course, it's not for those who like Windows, though, as there's a learning curve for macOS.

Anyway, under the hood of this laptop is the Apple M2 Pro or Apple M2 Max CPU. These CPUs are crazy good, thanks to the power of Apple's integrated GPU. On 14-inch models, you can choose either a 10-core CPU with a 16-core GPU on the M2 Pro, a 12-core CPU with a 19-core CPU, or all the way up to a 12-core CPU and a crazy 30-core GPU. 16-inch models have 12-core CPUS, and up to a 38-core GPU. And since this is an Arm-based chip, you get superb performance on battery, and when plugged into power for tasks like video editing or photo editing (unlike Windows laptops.)

What else is good about these laptops is the design. You can choose either Silver or Space Gray, but these laptops are crazy thin and light for the power they offer. Of course, ports are limited (2x Thunderbolt, SD card reader, and HDMI), but the MacBook is 0.66 inches thick, and weighs about 4.7 pounds. The display is great, too, since these are Liquid Retina XDR displays that pack in incredible contact ratios and lots of pixels (3024x1964 on 14-inch models and 3456x2234 on 16-inch models).

Razer Blade 16
Best gaming laptop

Powerful specs to play the latest games

The Razer Blade 16 is packing powerful 55W Intel processor and Nvidia GeForce RTX 40 series graphics with up to 175W of power. It even has the option for a dual-mode mini-LED display.

Pros
  • It has a 24-core CPU and up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 with 175W if power
  • Great display for both gaming and content creation
  • 1080p webcam with Windows Hello is rare for gaming laptops
Cons
  • Very expensive
  • Somewhat bulky
$3000 at Best Buy

It's not in everyone's price range, but the best gaming laptop you can find at Best Buy right now is without a doubt the Razer Blade 16. This powerful machine comes with the latest and greatest specs, plus it's Razer's first 16-inch laptop.

The Razer Blade 16 comes with an Intel Core i9-13950HX, an incredibly powerful 55W processor with 24 cores and 32 threads. That gives you near desktop-level CPU performance and a core count that would be hard to imagine on a laptop just a couple of years ago. You can also go up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 Laptop GPU, giving you all the performance you could want for gaming. In addition, the laptop comes with up to 32GB of DDR5 RAM, which you can upgrade later, along with up to 2TB of SSD storage.

As Razer's first 16-inch laptop, the Blade 16 comes with a 16:10 aspect ratio and Quad HD+ resolution plus a super-smooth 240Hz refresh rate. That's the base model, but the RTX 4080 and RTX 4090 models have a dual-mode mini-IPS display, which can actually change its native resolution and refresh rate, from Ultra HD+ at 60Hz for creative work to Full HD+ at 240Hz for gaming. There's also a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello facial recognition, a rarity for gaming laptops, even premium ones.

Razer also delivers on the design front, with a very clean look that could almost fit in any environment if it weren't for the glowing Razer logo on the lid. It does have an RGB keyboard, though. The laptop starts at 5.4 pounds of weight, so it's not super light, but it makes sense for these specs. Ending with ports, the Blade 16 comes with two USB-C ports (one with Thunderbolt 4), three USB Type-A ports, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a full-size SD card reader.

The Razer Blade 16 isn't a cheap laptop, but it delivers the good on just about every front.

HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
Best HP laptop

Featuring a premium design, a 3:2 display, and high-end specs, the Spectre x360 13.5 is one of the best convertibles and laptops overall you can buy today.

Pros
  • A convertible form factor is more versatile than a typical laptop
  • Tall 3:2 display with optional OLED configuration
  • One of the best webcams on any laptop
Cons
  • Best Buy doesn't have all the configurations HP offers
  • It's not terribly light considering its size
$1500 at Best Buy $1750 at Best Buy (OLED)

It shouldn't come as a surprise that HP's Spectre laptops are fantastic, and the Spectre x360 13.5 is easily one of the best the company has ever made.

Performance-wise, it's packing 13th-generation processors from Intel, specifically an Intel Core i7-1355U. This is a very capable processor with 10 cores and 12 threads, and it can handle day-to-day tasks without any issues. In addition, this model comes with 16GB of RAM and an up to 1TB SSD, which is very much a high-end combination. Of course, it's not a gaming laptop, but it's great for everyday use.

As the name suggests, the HP Spectre x360 13.5 has a 13.5-inch screen, and it comes in the 3:2 aspect ratio, even taller than the 16:10 format many companies use. The base configuration comes in Full HD+ resolution, but higher-end models have a stellar 3K2K OLED panel, which is super sharp and gives you benefits like true blacks and more vibrant colors, making for a stunning visual experience for both work and media consumption. HP also uses one of the best webcams you can find on a laptop — a 5MP sensor with Windows Hello support.

The latest Spectre x360 models don't have the same bold design of years past, but you still get a premium dual-tone look in Nightfall Black and Nocturne Blue, with the former being especially striking. You can also opt for the more boring Natural Silver if you want to. The laptop comes in at 3.01 pounds, but that makes sense for a convertible laptop made of aluminum. You get a solid selection of ports, too, with two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader.

There haven't been many changes with the latest model compared to last year's, but it was already great, as we noted in our review. The added performance is just a nice bonus.

Dell XPS 13 Plus
Best Dell laptop
$1303 $1650 Save $347

The Dell XPS 13 Plus is a futuristic-looking laptop with top-tier performance and stunning display options packed into one of the coolest designs we've seen for a laptop.

Pros
  • Powerful Intel P-series processors
  • Fantastic display, including a 3.5K OLED option
  • Futuristic and modern design
Cons
  • That new design may take some getting used to
  • Only two Thunderbolt 4 ports
$1303 at Best Buy FHD $1700 at Best Buy 3.5K OLED $1950 at Best Buy 13th

The Dell XPS lineup includes some of the best laptops on the market, and the Dell XPS 13 Plus is arguably the cream of the crop.

There is a version of the XPS 13 Plus with 13th-generation Intel processors, but it's not yet available at Best Buy. The 12th-generation models come with up to an Intel Core i7-1260P, which gives you 12 cores and 16 threads, and the 13th-gen versions (with a Core i7-1360P) are pretty similar, only slightly faster. You're not missing out on much by going with last year's model if you can't wait, considering everything else is the same. The laptop also includes up to 32GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD.

The display options available are also great, with this being a 13.4-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio. The base model uses an IPS panel at Full HD+ resolution, but you have the option for a stunning 3.5K OLED panel, which gives you true blacks and vivid colors that are impossible to mimic on an IPS panel. A downside of this laptop is that it does come with a 720p webcam, something Dell refuses to move on from even on its premium laptops. At least it has Windows Hello facial recognition.

The Dell XPS 13 Plus still looks futuristic and very unique, thanks to a few design choices. First, the touchpad is invisible and seamless with the chassis of the laptop. Dell also removed the physical function keys, replacing them with touch-based buttons, and opted for a zero-lattice keyboard. It's very portable, too, weighing 2.77 pounds. A potential problem is the port selection, which includes just two Thunderbolt 4 ports. Dell includes some adapters for a headphone jack and USB Type-A to help with that, though you may end up needing a dock.

Even with that downside, the Dell XPS 13 Plus is an undeniably cool laptop with top-tier performance and a great display. Whether you buy the 12th-gen model or wait for the newer one, you'll have a great experience.

MacBook Air (M2)
The best Apple laptop

For students or just fans of macOS

$899 $1099 Save $200

The MacBook Air M2 is one of the best Apple laptops you can find at Best Buy. It has a fresh new design and more powerful performance while being just as efficient as the previous model, making it one of the best clamshell laptops around.

Pros
  • Top-tier performance and efficiency courtesy of the Apple M2 chip
  • Great display included in every configuration
  • Premium build quality in four different colors
Cons
  • Storage and RAM upgrades are expensive
  • Base storage configuration is slower than the others
$899 at Best Buy (13 inches) $1299 at Best Buy (15 inches)

It's hard to talk about the best laptops on the market without mentioning the MacBook Air, and with the latest iteration, Apple has made one of the most popular laptops in the world even better, making it our pick for the best Apple laptop at Best Buy.

The latest MacBook Air is powered by the Apple M2 chip, an 8-core Arm-based CPU that promises up to 18% more CPU performance and up to 35% more GPU performance compared to its predecessor. Apple Silicon is extremely efficient compared to Intel or AMD processors, too, so battery life will be fantastic here. At Best Buy, you can choose between an 8-core or 10-core GPU, and all the models come with 8GB of RAM and up to 512GB SSD. Springing for the higher-end model may be worth it mostly because of the SSD, which is much faster in the 512GB model.

Apple has stretched the screen compared to previous models, so it's slightly taller, and it's now 13.6 inches diagonally with a 2560x1664 resolution, which is very sharp for this size. There's even a 15-inch model now, that packs more pixels with a 2880x1864 resolution. That display does have a notch, though, housing the upgraded 1080p webcam, backed by the image signal processor in the Apple M2 to further enhance quality.

This iteration of the MacBook Air comes in a brand-new chassis that's more balanced and still incredibly light, starting at just 2.7 pounds. It also comes in new colors, Starlight and Midnight, in addition to Silver and Space Gray. As for ports, we get two USB4/Thunderbolt connections, a headphone jack with support for high-impedance headphones, and a MagSafe connector for fast charging. It's not a ton of ports, but you can use a dock or hub if you need more.

Aside from that shortcoming, this is a great laptop in almost every way and one we can easily recommend to most people.

Lenovo Yoga 9i (2023)
Best Lenovo laptop

The best laptop on the market

The 2023 Lenovo Yoga 9i adds 13th-generation Intel Core processors to one of the best laptops on the market, with stunning OLED displays and a premium, shiny design.

Pros
  • 13th-generation Intel Core i7
  • Stunning OLED display, with two versions available
  • Striking metal design with polished edges
Cons
  • Can't store the included pen
$1400 at Best Buy (2.8K OLED) $1650 at Best Buy (4K OLED)

If you've read our review of the Lenovo Yoga 9i, it should come as no surprise that it's featured here. It's arguably the best laptop on the market, blending versatility, performance, and a fantastic media experience.

Starting with performance, the 2023 Lenovo Yoga 9i is powered by a 13th-generation Intel Core i7-1360P, a 12-core, 16-thread processor that should handle all kinds of day-to-day tasks without any issues. It also comes with 16GB of RAM and up to a 1TB SSD, everything you need for a great experience.

The display is another highlight, being a 14-inch panel OLED with a 16:10 aspect ratio. There are two configurations available — one with 2.8K resolution and a 90Hz refresh rate and another with 4K resolution and a 60Hz refresh rate, so you can choose what you value more. I'd argue the 2.8K model makes more sense for most people, as 4K won't be that much sharper at this size, and there's more of a benefit with the extra smoothness of 90Hz. This laptop also includes a 1080p webcam by default, including Windows Hello support.

The Lenovo Yoga 9i is one of the most beautiful laptops on the market. It comes in Oatmeal and Storm Grey colorways, and both look fantastic, with shiny polished edges and smooth metal surfaces. It also weighs 3.09 pounds, so it's fairly portable for a 14-inch convertible made of aluminum. Coming around to the ports, there are two Thunderbolt 4 connections, one standard USB Type-C port alongside one USB Type-A, and a headphone jack. Compared to most premium ultrabooks of this size, that's not bad at all.

The Lenovo Yoga 9i is a fantastic laptop in almost every way and a very easy one to recommend. The 2023 model only really upgrades the performance, but nothing else needed to change for it to be great.

Source: Asus
Asus ROG Zephyrus M16 (2023)
Best Asus laptop

A great gaming laptop that's still decently light

$1500 $1950 Save $450

The Asus ROG Zephyrus M16 is a powerful gaming laptop with 13th-gen Intel Core processors and Nvidia GeForce RTX 40 series graphics. It also has a great display, and it's decently light for what it offers.

Pros
  • Powerful 13th-gen Intel processors and Nvidia RTX graphics
  • Quad HD+ 240Hz display, including a mini-LED option
  • Relatively light for what it offers
Cons
  • Only the RTX 4070 model is currently available
  • It can get a bit expensive
$1500 at Best Buy (RTX 4070) $2700 at Best Buy (RTX 4080) $3300 at Best Buy (RTX 4090)

Asus makes some of the best gaming laptops on the market, and if you're shopping around at Best Buy, the ROG Zephyrus M16 is one of the best you can buy.

Starting with performance, the Asus ROG Zephyrus M16 comes with a 13th-generation Intel Core i9-13900H processor, with 14 cores and 20 threads delivering excellent performance for gaming and day-to-day use. It also comes with up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 Laptop GPU, though only the RTX 4070 model is currently available in new condition at Best Buy. Still, with 140W of power, you're getting a ton of performance here. You also get 16GB of RAM (which is upgradeable) and a 1TB SSD.

Things also look great in the display department, which a 16-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, Quad HD+ resolution, and a smooth 240Hz refresh rate. All models also include 100% coverage of DCI-P3, but while the RTX 4070 model comes with a standard IPS panel, the higher-end versions have a mini-LED panel with hundreds of dimming zones for an even better viewing experience. And above that display is a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support, which is great to see.

For the power it offers, the Asus ROG Zephyrus M16 is fairly light, starting at 4.63 pounds, and it looks very sleek, too. It's an all-black chassis, though there's a set of LED lights on the lid that can be customized with monochrome images and animations. The keyboard also supports single-zone RGB lighting. As for ports, we're looking at two USB-C ports (one supporting Thunderbolt 4), two USB Type-A ports, HDMI, and a 3.5mm headphone jack.

While it's a bit much for most people, the Asus ROG Zephyrus M16 is a great laptop with a lot of performance and a great display. If you're into gaming, you can't go wrong here.

Acer Chromebook Vero 514
Best laptop under $500

A solid experience for very cheap

The Acer Chromebook Vero 514 offers solid entry-level performance and a decent display in an eco-friendly design that looks different from anything else on the market.

Pros
  • A sustainable and eco-friendly design
  • Very capable specs for the price
  • Decent supply of ports
Cons
  • Chrome OS may not be good enough for everyone
  • Not as fancy or portable as more premium laptops
$500 at Best Buy

We can't all afford to spend nearly $1,000 on a new laptop, and the Acer Chromebook Vero 514 is a fantastic option if you're looking for something affordable. Yes, it's a Chromebook, so it might not be ideal if you're accustomed to Windows laptops, but for most people, this laptop can do anything you need it to do.

Performance-wise, the Acer Chromebook Vero 514 is powered by 12th-generation Intel Core processors, specifically a Core i3-1215U. That gives you six cores and eight threads for solid performance in all kinds of day-to-day tasks, whether you're browsing the web, watching videos, or even playing some lighter games. It also comes with 8GB of RAM and a 128GB SSD for storage, which is quite good for a Chromebook.

The display is also not too shabby. This is a 14-inch IPS panel with a typical 16:9 aspect ratio, which is still the most prevalent format in this price range. It comes in Full HD resolution, so it's a pretty sharp screen for its size, and it should make for a good visual experience overall. Above that display is a 1080p webcam, a rare sight for such an affordable laptop, but a welcome one, no doubt.

As part of Acer's Vero lineup, the Chromebook Pro 514 has a very environmentally-conscious design philosophy. The chassis uses unpainted recycled plastic materials, and while it's certainly not for everyone, this rough texture look definitely helps it stand out from the crowd. The laptop weighs in at 3.09 pounds, so it's not particularly light for its size, but it's still decently portable. It also comes with a decent selection of ports. You get two USB Type-C ports, one USB Type-A, HDMI, and a headphone jack, so all the basics are covered and all your peripherals should work out of the box without needing adapters.

All of that comes in at just under $500, and that's not including any discounts, which can make this an even better deal. At this price range, you can't ask for much more.

Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
Best Samsung laptop

A premium clamshell laptop with a stunning AMOLED display

$1264 $1450 Save $186

The Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is a fantastic premium laptop with 13th-gen Intel processors, a sturdy design, and a beautiful AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate.

Pros
  • Stunning AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate
  • Solid port selection for a premium laptop
  • 13th-generation Intel Core processors
Cons
  • A bit pricy
  • HDMI port only supports 4K at 30Hz
$1264 at Best Buy (14 inches) $1450 at Best Buy (16 inches)

Samsung is primarily known for its smartphones and the surrounding peripherals, but the company has also started making some of the best laptops you can buy each year. With the Galaxy Book 3 Pro, that's more true than ever.

For starters, the Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro features Intel's 13th-generation Intel Core processors, specifically with a Core i7-1360P. This 28W processor has 12 cores and 16 threads, delivering fantastic performance in day-to-day tasks. That processor is backed up by 16GB of RAM and up to 1TB of SSD storage, making for a great experience overall.

The real highlight, however, is the display. The laptop comes in both 14-inch and 16-inch flavors, but both feature a super-sharp 16:10 Dynamic AMOLED 2X panel. The resolution is 2880x1800, which looks fantastic in either size, and you also get a smooth 120Hz refresh rate that makes motion and animations look a lot nicer. Plus the usual benefits of OLED, like deep blacks and vibrant colors. There's a 1080p camera above that display delivering solid video quality, though Windows Hello is only supported via a fingerprint reader.

This iteration also comes with an all-aluminum design that feels very premium and solid. The laptop is still incredibly thin, but it's a lot heavier now. The 14-inch version comes in at 2.58 pounds, while the 16-inch model weighs 3.4 pounds. That's the trade-off for using aluminum, which feels more premium than the magnesium of previous models. Arguably one of the highlights of this laptop is the port selection, which includes two Thunderbolt 4 ports, one USB Type-A port, HDMI, and a headphone jack. The HDMI port is limited to 4K at 30Hz, but it's nice to have the option, at least.

With a fantastic display, high-end performance, and a premium design, the Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is one of the best laptops you can buy right now.

Best laptops at Best Buy in 2023: Bottom line

That gives you a few fantastic options if you're shopping for a laptop at Best Buy. All of these are high-end, premium devices that will give you a great experience. Arguably the best one of the bunch is the MacBook Pro. This laptop packs in the efficiency of the Apple M2 silicon under the hood, and it has a great design. It also comes with different screen sizes.

Apple MacBook Pro (2023)
The best overall laptop at Best Buy

For those who need the best possible laptop

$1799 $1999 Save $200

The best possible laptop you can buy at Best Buy is the Apple MacBook Pro (2023). This laptop comes in 14-inch and 16-inch sizes, and comes with the M2 Pro or M2 Max chips, ideal for video and photo editing, and for those who own other Apple devices.

$1799 at Best Buy (14 inches) $2299 at Best Buy (16 inches)

If you can't afford to spend that much, the Acer Chromebook Vero 514 is a great option for those that don't need Windows. It runs ChromeOS, and the specs it has are more than good enough to give you a great experience for day-to-day use, such as browsing the web and writing up documents. It all costs under $500, so it's great if you have limited funds.

With that being said, all of these are great laptops with a lot to give. In the case of the Windows laptops, they're all shipping with Windows 11 and you can get future updates, such as version 22H2, for free, so they'll last you a while longer. If you're not finding what you're looking for here, check out the best AMD Ryzen laptops, since the company didn't make many appearances on this list.

FAQ

Q: What is the return policy for laptops at Best Buy?

Of course, if you're shopping at Best Buy, it's important to know that you can count on a good return policy in case something goers wrong. The return policy at Best Buy gives you some room to breathe.

Most products are returnable within a 15-day period after the purchase for all customers. If you have a My Best Buy Plus or My Best Buy Total membership, that period is extended up to 60 days, which gives you even more time to find potential defects or realize the laptop you got isn't for you.

You can return the laptop you bought to a retail location or mail it back to Best Buy for free for a full refund, but the condition is that the laptop must be in like-new condition. If the laptop is visibly damaged, unsanitary, or missing any of the included accessories, Best Buy may deny the return. A return may also be denied if you're missing proof of purchase, or some fees, such as state taxes, may not be refunded if you return the product without proof of purchase.

Finally, "final sale" items are non-returnable, so if you bought an item on clearance, you may not be able to return it.
item.description:
Best Buy is one of the best places to go if you want to buy any kind of electronic device, including laptops. Not only does the company offer a huge selection of products, but its Price Match Guarantee helps you get the best deal. There are some exceptions to that, but regardless, this makes Best Buy a compelling place to visit if you want to get the best deal on a new laptop. Not only are you getting them from a reputable retailer, but you can also get them at one of the lowest prices available on the market.

While Best Buy does have a large selection of products, not every laptop on our best laptops list is available there. Generally, it's the more business-oriented products that may not show up there. For one reason or another, there isn't a limitless selection, and you'll still see laptops with RTX 40-series graphics cards, for example. So, in this round-up, we've gathered the best laptops you can buy at Best Buy.

  • Apple MacBook Pro (2023)
    The best overall laptop at Best Buy
    $1799 at Best Buy (14 inches)
  • Razer Blade 16
    Best gaming laptop
    $3000 at Best Buy
  • HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
    Best HP laptop
    $1500 at Best Buy
  • Dell XPS 13 Plus
    Best Dell laptop
    $1303 at Best Buy FHD
  • MacBook Air (M2)
    The best Apple laptop
    $899 at Best Buy (13 inches)
  • Lenovo Yoga 9i (2023)
    Best Lenovo laptop
    $1400 at Best Buy (2.8K OLED)
  • Source: Asus
    Asus ROG Zephyrus M16 (2023)
    Best Asus laptop
    $1500 at Best Buy (RTX 4070)
  • Acer Chromebook Vero 514
    Best laptop under $500
    $500 at Best Buy

    Best Samsung laptop
    $1264 at Best Buy (14 inches)

The top laptops available at Best Buy in 2023

Apple MacBook Pro (2023)
The best overall laptop at Best Buy

For those who need the best possible laptop

$1799 $1999 Save $200

The best possible laptop you can buy at Best Buy is the Apple MacBook Pro (2023). This laptop comes in 14-inch and 16-inch sizes, and comes with the M2 Pro or M2 Max chips, ideal for video and photo editing, and for those who own other Apple devices.

Pros
  • The Apple M2 chip is powerful
  • Great for those who own other Apple devices
  • A great display and design
Cons
  • Not for those who like Windows
  • Screen has a notch
$1799 at Best Buy (14 inches) $2299 at Best Buy (16 inches)

The best possible laptop you can find at Best Buy is also one of the most popular. It's the Apple MacBook Pro (2023). Though this laptop has a high cost, it packs plenty of value for the money. The laptop comes in both 14-inch and 16-inch sizes, for one, has an Arm-based SoC under the hood, built and designed by Apple. Even the display is great, though there's a notch. Of course, it's not for those who like Windows, though, as there's a learning curve for macOS.

Anyway, under the hood of this laptop is the Apple M2 Pro or Apple M2 Max CPU. These CPUs are crazy good, thanks to the power of Apple's integrated GPU. On 14-inch models, you can choose either a 10-core CPU with a 16-core GPU on the M2 Pro, a 12-core CPU with a 19-core CPU, or all the way up to a 12-core CPU and a crazy 30-core GPU. 16-inch models have 12-core CPUS, and up to a 38-core GPU. And since this is an Arm-based chip, you get superb performance on battery, and when plugged into power for tasks like video editing or photo editing (unlike Windows laptops.)

What else is good about these laptops is the design. You can choose either Silver or Space Gray, but these laptops are crazy thin and light for the power they offer. Of course, ports are limited (2x Thunderbolt, SD card reader, and HDMI), but the MacBook is 0.66 inches thick, and weighs about 4.7 pounds. The display is great, too, since these are Liquid Retina XDR displays that pack in incredible contact ratios and lots of pixels (3024x1964 on 14-inch models and 3456x2234 on 16-inch models).

Razer Blade 16
Best gaming laptop

Powerful specs to play the latest games

The Razer Blade 16 is packing powerful 55W Intel processor and Nvidia GeForce RTX 40 series graphics with up to 175W of power. It even has the option for a dual-mode mini-LED display.

Pros
  • It has a 24-core CPU and up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 with 175W if power
  • Great display for both gaming and content creation
  • 1080p webcam with Windows Hello is rare for gaming laptops
Cons
  • Very expensive
  • Somewhat bulky
$3000 at Best Buy

It's not in everyone's price range, but the best gaming laptop you can find at Best Buy right now is without a doubt the Razer Blade 16. This powerful machine comes with the latest and greatest specs, plus it's Razer's first 16-inch laptop.

The Razer Blade 16 comes with an Intel Core i9-13950HX, an incredibly powerful 55W processor with 24 cores and 32 threads. That gives you near desktop-level CPU performance and a core count that would be hard to imagine on a laptop just a couple of years ago. You can also go up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 Laptop GPU, giving you all the performance you could want for gaming. In addition, the laptop comes with up to 32GB of DDR5 RAM, which you can upgrade later, along with up to 2TB of SSD storage.

As Razer's first 16-inch laptop, the Blade 16 comes with a 16:10 aspect ratio and Quad HD+ resolution plus a super-smooth 240Hz refresh rate. That's the base model, but the RTX 4080 and RTX 4090 models have a dual-mode mini-IPS display, which can actually change its native resolution and refresh rate, from Ultra HD+ at 60Hz for creative work to Full HD+ at 240Hz for gaming. There's also a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello facial recognition, a rarity for gaming laptops, even premium ones.

Razer also delivers on the design front, with a very clean look that could almost fit in any environment if it weren't for the glowing Razer logo on the lid. It does have an RGB keyboard, though. The laptop starts at 5.4 pounds of weight, so it's not super light, but it makes sense for these specs. Ending with ports, the Blade 16 comes with two USB-C ports (one with Thunderbolt 4), three USB Type-A ports, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a full-size SD card reader.

The Razer Blade 16 isn't a cheap laptop, but it delivers the good on just about every front.

HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
Best HP laptop

Featuring a premium design, a 3:2 display, and high-end specs, the Spectre x360 13.5 is one of the best convertibles and laptops overall you can buy today.
